@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+name: Collect model data
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: "0 21 * * *"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  collect:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    permissions:
+      contents: write
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+        with:
+          python-version: "3.11"
+          cache: "pip"
+
+      - name: Install Python dependencies
+        run: |
+          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
+          pip install -r requirements.txt
+
+      - name: Collect model data
+        env:
+          OPENROUTER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY }}
+          HF_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HF_TOKEN }}
+        run: python scripts/collect_all.py
+
+      - name: Commit updated data
+        run: |
+          git config user.name "github-actions"
+          git config user.email "github-actions@github.com"
+          git add data/
+          git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "Update model data"
+          git push
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Trigger the Pages deploy after data pushes**

In the scheduled collect workflow, this `git push` uses the checkout credential, which is the repository `GITHUB_TOKEN` by default. GitHub documents that events triggered by `GITHUB_TOKEN` pushes "will not create a new workflow run" ([docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/security/github_token#when-github_token-triggers-workflow-runs)), and `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` only deploys on `push` to `main` or manual dispatch, so daily data commits will not update GitHub Pages until a human push/manual deploy happens. Trigger deploy from `workflow_run`/the same workflow, or push with a token intended to trigger downstream workflows.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid overwriting data when HF collection fails**

Because `hf_data` is consumed here without validating that the critical Hugging Face collection succeeded, a transient outage/rate limit where every HF query returns non-200 produces `normalized_models == []`; the script then writes empty `data/latest/*.json` files and the scheduled workflow commits them, effectively wiping the public dashboard. Since HF is the only base source for models, fail the job or keep the previous snapshot when `hf_data` is empty or unexpectedly small.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Format the timestamp in the timezone being labeled**

This formats `collected_at` in the viewer's browser timezone but then appends `JST`, so anyone outside Japan sees a mislabeled Last Update time (for example UTC/US browsers display their local hour as if it were Japan time). Pass `timeZone: 'Asia/Tokyo'` in the formatter or label it as the user's local timezone.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Move the responsive rule out of inline styles**

React's `style` prop does not evaluate nested `@media` rules, so this breakpoint never applies and the Home layout keeps `gridTemplateColumns: '1fr 320px'` even below 960px, causing the side column to squeeze/overflow on narrow screens. Put this rule in a CSS class or compute the style in JS based on viewport state.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+import re
+import math
+from datetime import datetime
+from dateutil import parser as date_parser
+
+class ModelNormalizer:
+    """逡ｰ縺ｪ繧九ョ繝ｼ繧ｿ繧ｽ繝ｼ繧ｹ縺九ｉ蜿朱寔縺輔ｌ縺溘Δ繝・Ν諠・ｱ繧堤ｵｱ蜷医・豁｣隕丞喧縺励―n+    蜈ｱ騾壹・繧ｹ繧ｭ繝ｼ繝槭→Radar Score繧堤ｮ怜・縺吶ｋ繧ｯ繝ｩ繧ｹ縲・n+    """
+    
+    def __init__(self, previous_models=None):
+        # 驕主悉縺ｮ繝｢繝・Ν繝・・繧ｿ・亥ｷｮ蛻・ｨ育ｮ励ｄ諤･荳頑・蛻､螳夂畑・噂n+        # {model_id: model_dict}
+        self.previous_models = previous_models or {}
+        self.current_time = datetime.utcnow()
+        
+    def normalize_all(self, hf_models, or_models, ol_models):
+        """Hugging Face, OpenRouter, Ollama縺ｮ蜈ｨ繝・・繧ｿ繧偵・繝ｼ繧ｸ繝ｻ豁｣隕丞喧縺吶ｋ"""
+        normalized_list = []
+        
+        # 1. Hugging Face繝｢繝・Ν繧偵・繝ｼ繧ｹ縺ｨ縺励※蛻晄悄蛹暴n+        for hf in hf_models:
+            model_id = hf["model_id"]
+            
+            # 繝｢繝・Ν蜷咲ｭ峨・繝代・繧ｹ
+            parsed = self._parse_model_info(model_id, hf.get("tags", []))
+            
+            # Ollama蟇ｾ蠢懃憾豕√・謗ｨ螳壹・邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            has_ollama, ollama_url = self._match_ollama(model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], ol_models)
+            
+            # OpenRouter縺ｮ陬懷勧諠・ｱ縺ｮ邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            or_info = self._match_openrouter(model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], or_models)
+            
+            # 逕ｨ騾斐ち繧ｰ莉倥￠
+            use_cases = self._determine_use_cases(model_id, hf.get("tags", []), parsed["size"])
+            
+            # 驕主悉縺ｮ螻･豁ｴ繝・・繧ｿ縺ｮ蜿ら・
+            prev_model = self.previous_models.get(model_id, {})
+            first_seen_at = prev_model.get("first_seen_at")
+            if not first_seen_at:
+                first_seen_at = hf.get("collected_at")
+                
+            # 繝｡繧ｿ繝・・繧ｿ讒狗ｯ噂n+            item = {
+                "id": model_id,
+                "display_name": parsed["display_name"],
+                "source": "huggingface",
+                "sources": {
+                    "huggingface": hf["model_url"],
+                    "ollama": ollama_url,
+                    "openrouter": or_info.get("model_url")
+                },
+                "author": hf["author"],
+                "model_family": parsed["family"],
+                "model_size": parsed["size"],
+                "quantization": parsed["quantization"],
+                "license": hf["license"] or or_info.get("license") or "unknown",
+                "downloads": hf["downloads"],
+                "likes": hf["likes"],
+                "last_modified": hf["last_modified"],
+                "first_seen_at": first_seen_at,
+                "last_seen_at": hf["collected_at"],
+                "has_gguf": hf["has_gguf"],
+                "has_ollama": has_ollama,
+                "context_length": or_info.get("context_length"),
+                "pricing": or_info.get("pricing"),
+                "description": or_info.get("description"),
+                "use_cases": use_cases,
+                "tags": hf.get("tags", []),
+                "collected_at": hf["collected_at"]
+            }
+            
+            # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢繝ｪ繝ｳ繧ｰ縺ｨ諤･荳頑・繝ｻ譁ｰ隕丞愛螳喀n+            item["radar_score"] = self._calculate_radar_score(item, prev_model)
+            item["is_new"] = self._determine_is_new(item)
+            item["is_rising"] = self._determine_is_rising(item, prev_model)
+            
+            normalized_list.append(item)
+            
+        # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢鬆・↓繧ｽ繝ｼ繝・n+        normalized_list.sort(key=lambda x: x["radar_score"], reverse=True)
+        return normalized_list
+
+    def _parse_model_info(self, model_id, tags):
+        """繝｢繝・ΝID繧・ち繧ｰ縺九ｉ縲√ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縲√し繧､繧ｺ縲・㍼蟄仙喧縺ｪ縺ｩ縺ｮ諠・ｱ繧呈歓蜃ｺ縺吶ｋ"""
+        parts = model_id.split("/")
+        name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else model_id
+        
+        # 1. 謗ｨ螳壹ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｮ迚ｹ螳喀n+        family = "Other"
+        family_map = {
+            "qwen": "Qwen",
+            "llama": "Llama",
+            "gemma": "Gemma",
+            "deepseek": "DeepSeek",
+            "mistral": "Mistral",
+            "phi": "Phi",
+            "smollm": "SmolLM",
+            "command-r": "Command-R",
+            "nemotron": "Nemotron",
+            "exallama": "Llama"
+        }
+        for kw, fam in family_map.items():
+            if kw in model_id.lower():
+                family = fam
+                break
+                
+        # 2. 謗ｨ螳壹Δ繝・Ν繧ｵ繧､繧ｺ縺ｮ迚ｹ螳・(萓・ 7b, 8b, 1.5b, 70b)
+        size = "unknown"
+        size_match = re.search(r'(?:^|[^a-zA-Z])(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB](?:[^a-zA-Z]|$)', name)
+        if size_match:
+            size = f"{size_match.group(1).upper()}B"
+        else:
+            # 繧ｿ繧ｰ縺九ｉ謗｢縺兔n+            for t in tags:
+                tag_match = re.search(r'^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB]$', str(t))
+                if tag_match:
+                    size = f"{tag_match.group(1).upper()}B"
+                    break
+                    
+        # 3. 驥丞ｭ仙喧蠖｢蠑上・迚ｹ螳喀n+        quant = "FP16" # 繝・ヵ繧ｩ繝ｫ繝・n+        if "gguf" in model_id.lower() or "gguf" in tags:
+            quant = "GGUF"
+        elif "gptq" in model_id.lower() or "gptq" in tags:
+            quant = "GPTQ"
+        elif "awq" in model_id.lower() or "awq" in tags:
+            quant = "AWQ"
+        elif "exl2" in model_id.lower():
+            quant = "EXL2"
+        else:
+            for t in tags:
+                if "quantized" in str(t).lower():
+                    quant = "Quantized"
+                    break
+                    
+        # 4. 陦ｨ遉ｺ蜷阪・謨ｴ蠖｢
+        # Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-GGUF -> Qwen2.5 7B Instruct GGUF
+        display_name = name.replace("-", " ").replace("_", " ")
+        # 驥崎､・☆繧九Ρ繝ｼ繝峨ｒ謨ｴ逅・ｼ井ｾ・ Qwen Qwen2.5 -> Qwen2.5・噂n+        display_name = " ".join(dict.fromkeys(display_name.split()))
+        
+        return {
+            "display_name": display_name,
+            "family": family,
+            "size": size,
+            "quantization": quant
+        }
+
+    def _match_ollama(self, model_id, family, size, ol_models):
+        """Ollama繧ｫ繧ｿ繝ｭ繧ｰ縺ｨ縺ｮ繝槭ャ繝√Φ繧ｰ繧定｡後＞縲∝ｯｾ蠢懃憾豕√→URL繧定ｿ斐☆"""
+        model_id_lower = model_id.lower()
+        
+        # 1. 繝｢繝・ΝID繧・ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縲√し繧､繧ｺ縺悟粋閾ｴ縺吶ｋ縺狗｢ｺ隱構n+        for ol in ol_models:
+            ol_name = ol["model_name"]
+            # 螳悟・繝槭ャ繝√√∪縺溘・繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｨ繧ｵ繧､繧ｺ縺御ｸ閾ｴ縺吶ｋ蝣ｴ蜷・n+            if ol_name in model_id_lower:
+                return True, ol["ollama_url"]
+            if ol["family"] == family and size != "unknown":
+                # Ollama縺ｮsizes縺ｫ "8B" 縺ｪ縺ｩ縺悟性縺ｾ繧後ｋ縺欺n+                ol_sizes = [s.upper() for s in ol.get("sizes", [])]
+                if size in ol_sizes:
+                    return True, ol["ollama_url"]
+

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Require a stronger OpenRouter match**

This accepts any OpenRouter model with two shared hyphen/slash tokens, which is too weak for model IDs that include other model names or generic tokens; for example the generated dataset maps a Qwen HF model containing `Claude-4.6-Opus` in its name to `anthropic/claude-opus-4.6`, and several 7B/12B models inherit context/pricing links from unrelated 24B/26B/72B OpenRouter entries. Require the same family and compatible size/base model before attaching supplemental OpenRouter metadata.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Do not mark guessed Ollama links as available**

When a HF model does not match the curated Ollama catalog, this fallback still returns `has_ollama: true` with a synthesized family URL for many known families under 72B, so arbitrary fine-tunes or unreleased variants are shown as Ollama-available and get the local-availability score boost. Keep `has_ollama` false unless the model is actually present in the catalog (or store the fallback as an unverified suggestion separately).

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep the main layout usable on phones**

With the sidebar always fixed at `--sidebar-width` and the main pane always offset by that width plus 48px horizontal padding, phone-sized viewports are left with almost no usable content width (for example 375px wide leaves 115px before padding). Add a small-screen layout that collapses or moves the sidebar instead of applying this desktop offset unconditionally.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match family names on token boundaries**

Because the family detector uses raw substring matching, any model ID containing `phi` inside another word is classified as Phi; the generated dataset already misclassifies `dphn/dolphin-2.9.1-yi-1.5-34b` this way, which then pollutes family filters/trends and downstream availability matching. Use token/word-boundary matching for short keys like `phi` instead of `kw in model_id.lower()`.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+            print(f"Warning: Failed to load previous models: {e}")
+    return {}
+
+def build_family_trends(models):
+    """繝｢繝・Ν繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ蛻･縺ｮ髮・ｨ医ョ繝ｼ繧ｿ繧堤函謌舌☆繧欺

"
+    families = {}
+    for m in models:
+        fam = m.get("model_family", "Other")
+        if fam not in families:
+            families[fam] = {
+                "family": fam,
+                "model_count": 0,
+                "new_model_count": 0,
+                "rising_model_count": 0,
+                "total_downloads": 0,
+                "total_likes": 0,
+                "radar_scores": []
+            }
+            
+        f_data = families[fam]
+        f_data["model_count"] += 1
+        if m.get("is_new"):
+            f_data["new_model_count"] += 1
+        if m.get("is_rising"):
+            f_data["rising_model_count"] += 1
+        f_data["total_downloads"] += m.get("downloads", 0)
+        f_data["total_likes"] += m.get("likes", 0)
+        f_data["radar_scores"].append(m.get("radar_score", 0.0))
+        
+    # 蟷ｳ蝮・､縺ｮ險育ｮ励→謨ｴ蠖｢
+    result = []
+    for fam, data in families.items():
+        scores = data["radar_scores"]
+        avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores) if scores else 0.0
+        del data["radar_scores"]
+        data["avg_radar_score"] = round(avg_score, 1)
+        result.append(data)
+        
+    # 繝｢繝・Ν繧ｫ繧ｦ繝ｳ繝磯・↓繧ｽ繝ｼ繝・n+    result.sort(key=lambda x: x["model_count"], reverse=True)
+    return result
+
+def main():
+    # 螳溯｡後ョ繧｣繝ｬ繧ｯ繝医Μ遲峨・貅門ｙ
+    base_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
+    data_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, "data")
+    latest_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, "latest")
+    history_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, "history")
+    
+    os.makedirs(latest_dir, exist_ok=True)
+    os.makedirs(history_dir, exist_ok=True)
+    
+    # 迺ｰ蠅・､画焚縺九ｉ繝医・繧ｯ繝ｳ遲峨・蜿門ｾ予n+    hf_token = os.environ.get("HF_TOKEN")
+    or_api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
+    
+    # 蜑榊屓縺ｮ繝・・繧ｿ縺ｮ繝ｭ繝ｼ繝噂n+    previous_models = load_previous_models(data_dir)
+    print(f"Loaded {len(previous_models)} historical models for delta analysis.")
+    
+    # 繝・・繧ｿ蜿朱寔
+    hf_collector = HuggingFaceCollector(token=hf_token)
+    or_collector = OpenRouterCollector(api_key=or_api_key)
+    ol_collector = OllamaLibraryCollector()
+    
+    hf_data = hf_collector.collect()
+    or_data = or_collector.collect()
+    ol_data = ol_collector.collect()
+    
+    # 繝・・繧ｿ豁｣隕丞喧縺ｨ邨ｱ蜷・n+    normalizer = ModelNormalizer(previous_models=previous_models)
+    normalized_models = normalizer.normalize_all(hf_data, or_data, ol_data)
+    
+    # 繝輔ぅ繝ｫ繧ｿ繝ｪ繝ｳ繧ｰ縺輔ｌ縺溘し繝悶そ繝・ヨ
+    rising_models = [m for m in normalized_models if m.get("is_rising")]
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Rank rising models by growth, not radar score**

`normalized_models` has already been sorted by `radar_score`, so filtering it here makes `rising.json`, the Home widget, and the Rising page's `RANK #` order reflect overall score rather than the download/like growth that made the models rising. Store the growth delta/rate during normalization and sort this subset by that metric before slicing `top_rising_models`.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

""逡ｰ縺ｪ繧九ョ繝ｼ繧ｿ繧ｽ繝ｼ繧ｹ縺九ｉ蜿朱寔縺輔ｌ縺溘Δ繝・Ν諠・ｱ繧堤ｵｱ蜷医・豁｣隕丞喧縺励―n+    蜈ｱ騾壹・繧ｹ繧ｭ繝ｼ繝槭→Radar Score繧堤ｮ怜・縺吶ｋ繧ｯ繝ｩ繧ｹ縲・n+    """
+    
+    def __init__(self, previous_models=None):
+        # 驕主悉縺ｮ繝｢繝・Ν繝・・繧ｿ・亥ｷｮ蛻・ｨ育ｮ励ｄ諤･荳頑・蛻､螳夂畑・噂n+        # {model_id: model_dict}
+        self.previous_models = previous_models or {}
+        self.current_time = datetime.utcnow()
+        
+    def normalize_all(self, hf_models, or_models, ol_models):
+        """Hugging Face, OpenRouter, Ollama縺ｮ蜈ｨ繝・・繧ｿ繧偵・繝ｼ繧ｸ繝ｻ豁｣隕丞喧縺吶ｋ"""
+        normalized_list = []
+        
+        # 1. Hugging Face繝｢繝・Ν繧偵・繝ｼ繧ｹ縺ｨ縺励※蛻晄悄蛹暴n+        for hf in hf_models:
+            model_id = hf["model_id"]
+            
+            # 繝｢繝・Ν蜷咲ｭ峨・繝代・繧ｹ
+            parsed = self._parse_model_info(model_id, hf.get("tags", []))
+            
+            # Ollama蟇ｾ蠢懃憾豕√・謗ｨ螳壹・邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            has_ollama, ollama_url = self._match_ollama(model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], ol_models)
+            
+            # OpenRouter縺ｮ陬懷勧諠・ｱ縺ｮ邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            or_info = self._match_openrouter(model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], or_models)
+            
+            # 逕ｨ騾斐ち繧ｰ莉倥￠
+            use_cases = self._determine_use_cases(model_id, hf.get("tags", []), parsed["size"])
+            
+            # 驕主悉縺ｮ螻･豁ｴ繝・・繧ｿ縺ｮ蜿ら・
+            prev_model = self.previous_models.get(model_id, {})
+            first_seen_at = prev_model.get("first_seen_at")
+            if not first_seen_at:
+                first_seen_at = hf.get("collected_at")
+                
+            # 繝｡繧ｿ繝・・繧ｿ讒狗ｯ噂n+            item = {
+                "id": model_id,
+                "display_name": parsed["display_name"],
+                "source": "huggingface",
+                "sources": {
+                    "huggingface": hf["model_url"],
+                    "ollama": ollama_url,
+                    "openrouter": or_info.get("model_url")
+                },
+                "author": hf["author"],
+                "model_family": parsed["family"],
+                "model_size": parsed["size"],
+                "quantization": parsed["quantization"],
+                "license": hf["license"] or or_info.get("license") or "unknown",
+                "downloads": hf["downloads"],
+                "likes": hf["likes"],
+                "last_modified": hf["last_modified"],
+                "first_seen_at": first_seen_at,
+                "last_seen_at": hf["collected_at"],
+                "has_gguf": hf["has_gguf"],
+                "has_ollama": has_ollama,
+                "context_length": or_info.get("context_length"),
+                "pricing": or_info.get("pricing"),
+                "description": or_info.get("description"),
+                "use_cases": use_cases,
+                "tags": hf.get("tags", []),
+                "collected_at": hf["collected_at"]
+            }
+            
+            # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢繝ｪ繝ｳ繧ｰ縺ｨ諤･荳頑・繝ｻ譁ｰ隕丞愛螳喀n+            item["radar_score"] = self._calculate_radar_score(item, prev_model)
+            item["is_new"] = self._determine_is_new(item)
+            item["is_rising"] = self._determine_is_rising(item, prev_model)
+            
+            normalized_list.append(item)
+            
+        # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢鬆・↓繧ｽ繝ｼ繝・n+        normalized_list.sort(key=lambda x: x["radar_score"], reverse=True)
+        return normalized_list
+
+    def _parse_model_info(self, model_id, tags):
+        """繝｢繝・ΝID繧・ち繧ｰ縺九ｉ縲√ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縲√し繧､繧ｺ縲・㍼蟄仙喧縺ｪ縺ｩ縺ｮ諠・ｱ繧呈歓蜃ｺ縺吶ｋ"""
+        parts = model_id.split("/")
+        name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else model_id
+        
+        # 1. 謗ｨ螳壹ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｮ迚ｹ螳喀n+        family = "Other"
+        family_map = {
+            "qwen": "Qwen",
+            "llama": "Llama",
+            "gemma": "Gemma",
+            "deepseek": "DeepSeek",
+            "mistral": "Mistral",
+            "phi": "Phi",
+            "smollm": "SmolLM",
+            "command-r": "Command-R",
+            "nemotron": "Nemotron",
+            "exallama": "Llama"
+        }
+        for kw, fam in family_map.items():
+            if kw in model_id.lower():
+                family = fam

+
+    def __init__(self, previous_models=None):

+
+            # 繝｢繝・Ν蜷咲ｭ峨・繝代・繧ｹ

+            )
+
+            # OpenRouter縺ｮ陬懷勧諠・ｱ縺ｮ邏蝉ｻ倥￠

+            )

+            # 逕ｨ騾斐ち繧ｰ莉倥￠
+            use_cases = self._determine_use_cases(
+                model_id, hf.get("tags", []), parsed["size"]

+            prev_model = self.previous_models.get(model_id, {})

+                dl_growth = (downloads - prev_dl) / prev_dl if prev_dl > 0 else 0.0
+                likes_growth = (
+                    (hf["likes"] - prev_likes) / prev_likes if prev_likes > 0 else 0.0
+                )
+                growth_rate = max(dl_growth, likes_growth)
+
+            # 繝｡繧ｿ繝・・繧ｿ讒狗ｯ噂n+            item = {

+                    "openrouter": or_info.get("model_url"),

+                "author": hf["author"],
+                "model_family": parsed["family"],

+                "quantization": parsed["quantization"],
+                "license": hf["license"] or or_info.get("license") or "unknown",
+                "downloads": hf["downloads"],

+                tag_match = re.search(r"^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB]$", str(t))
+                if tag_match:

+
+        # 3. 驥丞ｭ仙喧蠖｢蠑上・迚ｹ螳喀n+        quant = "FP16"  # 繝・ヵ繧ｩ繝ｫ繝・n+        if "gguf" in model_id.lower() or "gguf" in tags:

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Validate Hugging Face tasks before adding models**

This loop accepts every Hugging Face result without checking `pipeline_tag` or model tags against the local/open-weight LLM scope, so unrelated high-download models enter the dashboard when the API search returns them. The committed dataset already includes `openai/whisper-large-v3`, `google/vit-base-patch16-224`, and `Falconsai/nsfw_image_detection`, which pollute rankings, totals, and filters for a Local LLM dashboard.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Require a closer OpenRouter model-name match**

Matching OpenRouter rows by only family and size lets the first same-size variant/generation overwrite details for a different model. In the generated data, `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct-GGUF` links to `qwen/qwen3-vl-32b-instruct`, so the detail modal can show the wrong API link, context length, and description for affected models.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Parse million-parameter sizes before marking them unknown**

This size parser only recognizes `B` suffixes, so collected sub-billion models named with `M` units are marked as `unknown`. The committed data has `ggml-org/SmolVLM-500M-Instruct-GGUF` and `ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300M-GGUF` with `model_size: unknown`, which excludes them from the size filter and prevents the Lightweight tag/metadata score from reflecting their actual small size.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve Q-level quantization from GGUF names**

Treating any GGUF repository as `quantization: "GGUF"` conflates the container format with the actual quantization level. For models such as `...Q8_0-GGUF`, the detail modal reports only `GGUF` instead of the Q-level users need to estimate local memory/performance tradeoffs, even though the filename contains that information.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fail collection when JSON writes fail**

If any JSON output cannot be written, this handler only logs the exception and the script still exits successfully, so the collect workflow can commit/deploy stale, missing, or even partially truncated data after a disk/permission/write error. Treat these write failures as fatal before `git add data/` runs so the public dashboard is not updated with inconsistent datasets.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Move responsive rules out of React inline styles**

React's `style` prop does not evaluate nested `@media` keys, so this mobile fallback never applies. On narrow screens the modal still uses the two-column `1fr 300px` grid inside a padded dialog, which can push the stats/link column off-screen instead of stacking it below the main content.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

""Hugging Face API縺九ｉ繝ｭ繝ｼ繧ｫ繝ｫLLM蛟呵｣懊・繝｢繝・Ν諠・ｱ繧貞庶髮・☆繧九さ繝ｬ繧ｯ繧ｿ繝ｼ"""
+
+    def __init__(self, token=None):
+        self.token = token
+        self.headers = {}
+        if token:
+            self.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {token}"
+
+    def collect(self):
+        models_map = {}
+
+        # 讀懃ｴ｢譚｡莉ｶ縺ｮ螳夂ｾｩ
+        # GGUF繝｢繝・Ν縺翫ｈ縺ｳ荳ｻ隕√Δ繝・Ν繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｮ繝｢繝・Ν繧貞庶髮・☆繧欺n+        queries = [
+            {"tags": "gguf", "limit": 100},
+            {"search": "gguf", "limit": 100},
+            {"search": "qwen", "tags": "text-generation", "limit": 50},
+            {"search": "llama", "tags": "text-generation", "limit": 50},
body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter out non-LLM Hugging Face rows**

These queries depend on the `tags` parameter to restrict Hugging Face results, but the generated dataset from this commit already contains non-LLM/non-text-generation rows such as `openai/whisper-large-v3`, `FacebookAI/xlm-roberta-base`, and sentence-transformer embeddings with `has_gguf: false` and `model_family: Other`. When those popular rows are returned, they flow into models/new/summary and appear in the dashboard even though the product is scoped to local/open-weight LLMs, so add a supported HF filter or a response-side pipeline/GGUF/family guard before normalizing.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+        normalized_list = []
+

+            model_id = hf["model_id"]
+
+            # 繝｢繝・Ν蜷咲ｭ峨・繝代・繧ｹ

+            # Ollama蟇ｾ蠢懃憾豕√・謗ｨ螳壹・邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            has_ollama, ollama_url = self._match_ollama(
+                model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], ol_models
+            )
+
+            # OpenRouter縺ｮ陬懷勧諠・ｱ縺ｮ邏蝉ｻ倥￠
+            or_info = self._match_openrouter(
+                model_id, parsed["family"], parsed["size"], or_models
+            )
+
+            # 逕ｨ騾斐ち繧ｰ莉倥￠
+            use_cases = self._determine_use_cases(
+                model_id, hf.get("tags", []), parsed["size"]
+            )
+
+            # 驕主悉縺ｮ螻･豁ｴ繝・・繧ｿ縺ｮ蜿ら・
+            prev_model = self.previous_models.get(model_id, {})
+            first_seen_at = prev_model.get("first_seen_at")
+            if not first_seen_at:
+                first_seen_at = hf.get("collected_at")

+            # 謌宣聞邇・・險育ｮ予n+            downloads = hf["downloads"]

+                likes_growth = (

+                )
+                growth_rate = max(dl_growth, likes_growth)
+
+            # 繝｡繧ｿ繝・・繧ｿ讒狗ｯ噂n+            item = {

+                "display_name": parsed["display_name"],
+                "source": "huggingface",
+                "sources": {

+                    "openrouter": or_info.get("model_url"),

+                "model_family": parsed["family"],

+                "quantization": parsed["quantization"],
+                "license": hf["license"] or or_info.get("license") or "unknown",

+                "description": or_info.get("description"),
+                "use_cases": use_cases,
+                "tags": hf.get("tags", []),
+                "growth_rate": growth_rate,
+                "collected_at": hf["collected_at"],
+            }
+
+            # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢繝ｪ繝ｳ繧ｰ縺ｨ諤･荳頑・繝ｻ譁ｰ隕丞愛螳喀n+            item["radar_score"] = self._calculate_radar_score(item, prev_model)

+        family_map = {
+            "qwen": "Qwen",
+            "llama": "Llama",
+            "gemma": "Gemma",
+            "deepseek": "DeepSeek",

+            "phi": "Phi",

+        else:

+                tag_match = re.search(r"^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB]$", str(t))
+                if tag_match:
+                    size = f"{tag_match.group(1).upper()}B"
+                    break
+
+        # 3. 驥丞ｭ仙喧蠖｢蠑上・迚ｹ螳喀n+        quant = "FP16"  # 繝・ヵ繧ｩ繝ｫ繝・n+        if "gguf" in model_id.lower() or "gguf" in tags:
+            quant = "GGUF"
+        elif "gptq" in model_id.lower() or "gptq" in tags:
+            quant = "GPTQ"
+        elif "awq" in model_id.lower() or "awq" in tags:

+        else:

+                    quant = "Quantized"
+                    break
+
+        # 4. 陦ｨ遉ｺ蜷阪・謨ｴ蠖｢
+        # Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-GGUF -> Qwen2.5 7B Instruct GGUF
+        display_name = name.replace("-", " ").replace("_", " ")

+
+        return {

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Require stricter OpenRouter identity matches**

When multiple OpenRouter models share family and size, this first `fam_key in or_id` hit can attach an unrelated model; the committed output shows `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct-GGUF` linked to `qwen3-vl-32b-instruct` and `meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B` linked to `aion-rp-llama-3.1-8b`. That means the modal presents wrong context length/pricing/description and external API link, so match on a normalized model name/version rather than family+size alone.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid marking every same-size family model as Ollama-ready**

The family+size fallback marks any Hugging Face model whose ID contains the family name as available in the first Ollama catalog entry with that size; for example the committed data flags `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B` and `meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B` as `has_ollama` with the `llama3.1` library URL even though those are not that catalog item. This makes the Ollama filter and external links misleading for many fine-tunes/distills, so only return true on exact catalog-name/version aliases or a curated mapping.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+
+        # 讀懃ｴ｢譚｡莉ｶ縺ｮ螳夂ｾｩ

+            {"search": "deepseek", "tags": "text-generation", "limit": 50},
+            {"search": "mistral", "tags": "text-generation", "limit": 30},

+        ]
+
+        print("Starting Hugging Face data collection...")

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fail collection on partial Hugging Face query errors**

If one or more Hugging Face queries return non-200 or raise while another query still returns at least 20 rows, the collector only logs the failure and `collect_all.py` will overwrite `data/latest` with that partial catalog. A transient failure for a family query such as Qwen or Llama would silently remove those models from the dashboard until a later successful run, so propagate query failures or require all critical queries to succeed before writing outputs.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Use bounded checks for short language codes**

Because `ja` is tested as a raw substring of the full model ID, models whose author/name merely contains those letters are tagged as Japanese; the committed data includes `Jackrong/Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled-GGUF`, `janhq/Jan-v3.5-4B-gguf`, and `deepseek-community/Janus-Pro-1B` even though their tags do not include `ja` or `japanese`. This makes the Japanese filter return unrelated models, so only treat short language codes as exact tags or token-bounded name segments.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fail the collection when JSON writes fail**

If any output write fails, for example due to a full disk or permission problem in Actions, this catch only logs the error and the script still prints success and exits 0. The workflow can then commit a mixture of newly written, stale, or partially truncated JSON files, so the collection should abort with a nonzero exit as soon as a required dataset cannot be written.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Tighten Ollama matching before setting availability**

When a Hugging Face model only shares family and size with a catalog entry, this fallback marks it Ollama-ready even if that exact model is not in the Ollama library. In the committed data this marks unrelated models such as `nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2` as `https://ollama.com/library/llama3.2`, which makes the Ollama filter/source link and the local-availability part of `radar_score` incorrect; require a normalized catalog-name/version match before returning true.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Require a closer OpenRouter name match before merging**

For models that share family and size but are different checkpoints, this condition returns the first OpenRouter entry that passes the loose instruct check; in the checked-in data `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct-GGUF` is linked to `qwen/qwen3-vl-32b-instruct`, so context/pricing/descriptions and source links can point at a different model. Please match the normalized model name/version, and treat `-it` consistently, before copying OpenRouter metadata.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reject partial Hugging Face collections before writing**

If one Hugging Face query succeeds but the remaining queries fail or get rate-limited, `hf_data` can still be well above 20, so the scheduled workflow will write and commit a partial `latest/models.json` while dropping previously tracked models. The committed dataset has 402 entries, so a single 50/100-limit query is enough to pass this guard; abort on any query failure or compare against the previous count before overwriting the data files.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Scope mobile sidebar CSS to the real sidebar**

On screens <=768px this global `aside` selector also matches the Home page's right-column `<aside>` that contains the Rising/New widgets, so those cards are transformed off-canvas and fixed like the navigation drawer instead of appearing under the main column. Target the sidebar with a class rather than every `aside` element to keep the Home widgets visible on mobile.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Check DeepSeek before Qwen when assigning family**

Because `qwen` is checked before `deepseek`, official DeepSeek distilled checkpoints such as `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B` are categorized as Qwen in the generated data. That makes the DeepSeek family trend/filter undercount these models and puts them in the wrong family bucket; prefer the owner/base family or at least match `deepseek` before architecture names like Qwen/Llama.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match Japanese tags on token boundaries**

Using plain substring matching for `ja` tags models as Japanese whenever those two letters appear inside another word; the checked-in data already marks `Jackrong/Qwen3.5-...` as Japanese solely because the author name contains `ja`. This pollutes the Japanese use-case filter, so match `ja` as a separate token/tag or use an explicit language tag check.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Parse million-parameter model sizes**

The size parser only recognizes `B` suffixes, so sub-1B models with `M` suffixes are emitted as `model_size: "unknown"` (for example `ggml-org/SmolVLM-500M-Instruct-GGUF`, `google/gemma-3-270m`, and `facebook/opt-125m` in the checked-in data). Those models then disappear from the 0.1B-3B size filter, miss the Lightweight tag, and lose metadata score despite being exactly the local/lightweight models this dashboard is meant to surface.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Move mobile media rules out of inline styles**

React inline styles do not evaluate nested `@media` rules, so this intended mobile layout never applies; on narrow screens the search input and fixed-width sort control stay in one flex row and can overflow the Model Explorer filter panel. Put this breakpoint in CSS classes instead of the style object so the controls actually stack on mobile.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Prioritize DeepSeek before base-model tokens**

Because the first substring match wins and `qwen`/`llama` are checked before `deepseek`, DeepSeek distill models are counted under their base architecture instead of the DeepSeek family. In the generated data I checked, `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B` is classified as `Qwen` and `DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B` as `Llama`, which makes the Family Trends and family filter under-report DeepSeek releases. Put the model/product token ahead of base-family tokens or match the leading model name first.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match `ja` only as a language token**

The Japanese detector treats the two letters `ja` anywhere in the model id as Japanese, so unrelated names containing that substring are mislabeled. In the committed data this marks examples like `Jackrong/...`, `janhq/Jan-v3.5-4B-gguf`, and `...javanese...` with the Japanese use case, causing the Japanese filter and tags to show false positives; require `ja` to be a standalone language tag/token instead of a raw substring.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+            # 謌宣聞邇・・險育ｮ予n+            downloads = hf["downloads"]
+            growth_rate = 0.0
+            if prev_model:

+                prev_likes = prev_model.get("likes", 0)
+                dl_growth = (downloads - prev_dl) / prev_dl if prev_dl > 0 else 0.0
+                likes_growth = (
+                    (hf["likes"] - prev_likes) / prev_likes if prev_likes > 0 else 0.0
+                )
+                growth_rate = max(dl_growth, likes_growth)
+
+            # 繝｡繧ｿ繝・・繧ｿ讒狗ｯ噂n+            item = {
+                "id": model_id,
+                "display_name": parsed["display_name"],
+                "source": "huggingface",
+                "sources": {
+                    "huggingface": hf["model_url"],
+                    "ollama": ollama_url,
+                    "openrouter": or_info.get("model_url"),
+                },
+                "author": hf["author"],
+                "model_family": parsed["family"],
+                "model_size": parsed["size"],
+                "quantization": parsed["quantization"],
+                "license": hf["license"] or or_info.get("license") or "unknown",
+                "downloads": hf["downloads"],
+                "likes": hf["likes"],
+                "last_modified": hf["last_modified"],
+                "first_seen_at": first_seen_at,
+                "last_seen_at": hf["collected_at"],
+                "has_gguf": hf["has_gguf"],
+                "has_ollama": has_ollama,
+                "context_length": or_info.get("context_length"),
+                "pricing": or_info.get("pricing"),
+                "description": or_info.get("description"),
+                "use_cases": use_cases,
+                "tags": hf.get("tags", []),
+                "growth_rate": growth_rate,
+                "collected_at": hf["collected_at"],
+            }
+
+            # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢繝ｪ繝ｳ繧ｰ縺ｨ諤･荳頑・繝ｻ譁ｰ隕丞愛螳喀n+            item["radar_score"] = self._calculate_radar_score(item, prev_model)
+            item["is_new"] = self._determine_is_new(item)
+            item["is_rising"] = self._determine_is_rising(item, prev_model)
+
+            normalized_list.append(item)
+
+        # 繧ｹ繧ｳ繧｢鬆・↓繧ｽ繝ｼ繝・n+        normalized_list.sort(key=lambda x: x["radar_score"], reverse=True)
+        return normalized_list
+
+    def _parse_model_info(self, model_id, tags):
+        """繝｢繝・ΝID繧・ち繧ｰ縺九ｉ縲√ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縲√し繧､繧ｺ縲・㍼蟄仙喧縺ｪ縺ｩ縺ｮ諠・ｱ繧呈歓蜃ｺ縺吶ｋ"""
+        parts = model_id.split("/")
+        name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else model_id
+
+        # 1. 謗ｨ螳壹ヵ繧｡繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｮ迚ｹ螳喀n+        family = "Other"
+        family_map = {
+            "qwen": "Qwen",
+            "llama": "Llama",
+            "gemma": "Gemma",
+            "deepseek": "DeepSeek",
+            "mistral": "Mistral",
+            "phi": "Phi",
+            "smollm": "SmolLM",
+            "command-r": "Command-R",
+            "nemotron": "Nemotron",
+            "exallama": "Llama",
+        }
+        model_id_lower = model_id.lower()
+        for kw, fam in family_map.items():
+            if kw == "phi":
+                # phi縺ｯ迢ｬ遶九＠縺溷腰隱槫｢・阜縺ｧ繝槭ャ繝√☆繧具ｼ・olphin遲峨・驛ｨ蛻・ｸ閾ｴ繧帝亟豁｢・噂n+                if re.search(r"(?:^|[^a-zA-Z])phi(?:[^a-zA-Z]|$)", model_id_lower):
+                    family = fam
+                    break
+            elif kw in model_id_lower:
+                family = fam
+                break
+
+        # 2. 謗ｨ螳壹Δ繝・Ν繧ｵ繧､繧ｺ縺ｮ迚ｹ螳・(萓・ 7b, 8b, 1.5b, 70b)
+        size = "unknown"
+        size_match = re.search(
+            r"(?:^|[^a-zA-Z])(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB](?:[^a-zA-Z]|$)", name
+        )
+        if size_match:
+            size = f"{size_match.group(1).upper()}B"
+        else:
+            # 繧ｿ繧ｰ縺九ｉ謗｢縺兔n+            for t in tags:
+                tag_match = re.search(r"^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB]$", str(t))
+                if tag_match:
+                    size = f"{tag_match.group(1).upper()}B"
+                    break
+
+        # 3. 驥丞ｭ仙喧蠖｢蠑上・迚ｹ螳喀n+        quant = "FP16"  # 繝・ヵ繧ｩ繝ｫ繝・n+        if "gguf" in model_id.lower() or "gguf" in tags:
+            quant = "GGUF"
+        elif "gptq" in model_id.lower() or "gptq" in tags:
+            quant = "GPTQ"
+        elif "awq" in model_id.lower() or "awq" in tags:
+            quant = "AWQ"
+        elif "exl2" in model_id.lower():
+            quant = "EXL2"
+        else:

+
+        # 4. 陦ｨ遉ｺ蜷阪・謨ｴ蠖｢

+        display_name = name.replace("-", " ").replace("_", " ")
+        # 驥崎､・☆繧九Ρ繝ｼ繝峨ｒ謨ｴ逅・ｼ井ｾ・ Qwen Qwen2.5 -> Qwen2.5・噂n+        display_name = " ".join(dict.fromkeys(display_name.split()))
+
+        return {
+            "display_name": display_name,
+            "family": family,
+            "size": size,

+        }
+
+    def _match_ollama(self, model_id, family, size, ol_models):
+        """Ollama繧ｫ繧ｿ繝ｭ繧ｰ縺ｨ縺ｮ繝槭ャ繝√Φ繧ｰ繧定｡後＞縲∝ｯｾ蠢懃憾豕√→URL繧定ｿ斐☆"""
+        model_id_lower = model_id.lower()
+
+        # 髱咏噪繧ｫ繧ｿ繝ｭ繧ｰ縺ｫ遒ｺ螳溘↓蟄伜惠縺吶ｋ荳ｻ隕√Δ繝・Ν縺ｮ縺ｿ繧・Ollama 蟇ｾ蠢懊→縺吶ｋ
+        for ol in ol_models:
+            ol_name = ol["model_name"]
+
+            # 1. 繧ｫ繧ｿ繝ｭ繧ｰ繝｢繝・Ν蜷阪′繝｢繝・ΝID縺ｫ蜷ｫ縺ｾ繧後ｋ縺・(萓・ "llama3.2" in "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B")
+            if ol_name in model_id_lower:
+                if size != "unknown":
+                    ol_sizes = [s.upper() for s in ol.get("sizes", [])]
+                    if size in ol_sizes:
+                        return True, ol["ollama_url"]
+                else:
+                    return True, ol["ollama_url"]
+
+            # 2. 繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ繝ｼ縺ｨ繧ｵ繧､繧ｺ縺梧ｭ｣遒ｺ縺ｫ蜷郁・縺励√°縺､繝｢繝・ΝID縺ｫ繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ繝ｼ蜷阪′蜷ｫ縺ｾ繧後ｋ蝣ｴ蜷・n+            if ol["family"] == family and size != "unknown":
+                ol_sizes = [s.upper() for s in ol.get("sizes", [])]
+                if size in ol_sizes:
+                    if family.lower() in model_id_lower:
+                        return True, ol["ollama_url"]
+
+        return False, None
+
+    def _match_openrouter(self, model_id, family, size, or_models):
+        """OpenRouter繝｢繝・Ν諠・ｱ縺ｨ縺ｮ繝槭ャ繝√Φ繧ｰ"""
+        if family == "Other" or size == "unknown":
+            return {}
+
+        model_id_lower = model_id.lower()
+        fam_key = family.lower()
+        size_key = size.lower().replace("b", "")
+
+        for or_m in or_models:
+            or_id = or_m["model_id"].lower()
+
+            # 繝輔ぃ繝溘Μ繝ｼ蜷阪′蜷ｫ縺ｾ繧後√°縺､繧ｵ繧､繧ｺ縺御ｸ閾ｴ縺吶ｋ縺薙→繧貞ｿ・医→縺吶ｋ
+            if fam_key in or_id:
+                or_size_match = re.search(
+                    r"(?:^|[^a-zA-Z])(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)[bB](?:[^a-zA-Z]|$)", or_id
+                )
+                if or_size_match:
+                    or_size = or_size_match.group(1)
+                    if or_size == size_key:
+                        # 謖・ｰ弱ョ繝ｼ繧ｿ縺九←縺・°縺ｮ謨ｴ蜷域ｧ繧り・・
+                        is_instruct_hf = (
+                            "instruct" in model_id_lower
+                            or "chat" in model_id_lower
+                            or "it" in model_id_lower
+                        )
+                        is_instruct_or = "instruct" in or_id or "chat" in or_id
+
+                        if is_instruct_hf == is_instruct_or:
+                            return {
+                                "context_length": or_m.get("context_length"),
+                                "pricing": or_m.get("pricing"),
+                                "description": or_m.get("description"),
+                                "license": or_m.get("license"),
+                                "model_url": f"https://openrouter.ai/models/{or_m['model_id']}",
+                            }
+
+        return {}
+
+    def _determine_use_cases(self, model_id, tags, size):
+        """繝｢繝・ΝID縲√ち繧ｰ縲√し繧､繧ｺ縺九ｉ逕ｨ騾斐ｒ謗ｨ螳壹☆繧欺

"
+        use_cases = []
+        model_id_lower = model_id.lower()
+        tags_lower = [str(t).lower() for t in tags]
+
+        # 1. Coding
+        coder_kws = ["coder", "code", "coding", "instruct-code", "developer"]
+        if any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in coder_kws) or "code" in tags_lower:
+            use_cases.append("Coding")
+
+        # 2. Japanese
+        ja_kws = ["japanese", "ja", "nihongo"]
+        if (
+            any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in ja_kws)
+            or "japanese" in tags_lower
+            or "ja" in tags_lower
+        ):
+            use_cases.append("Japanese")
+
+        # 3. Reasoning
+        reasoning_kws = ["reasoning", "r1", "thinking", "math", "cot", "deepseek-r"]
+        if (
+            any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in reasoning_kws)
+            or "reasoning" in tags_lower
+        ):
+            use_cases.append("Reasoning")
+
+        # 4. RAG / Long-context
+        rag_kws = ["long-context", "rag", "128k", "32k", "embedding"]
+        if (
+            any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in rag_kws)
+            or "rag" in tags_lower
+            or "long-context" in tags_lower
+        ):
+            use_cases.append("RAG")
+
+        # 5. Lightweight
+        if size != "unknown":
+            try:
+                num_size = float(size.replace("B", ""))
+                if num_size <= 9.0:
+                    use_cases.append("Lightweight")
+            except ValueError:

+
+        # 6. Vision / Multimodal
+        vision_kws = ["vision", "vl", "multimodal", "image"]
+        if (
+            any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in vision_kws)
+            or "multimodal" in tags_lower

+        ):
+            use_cases.append("Vision")
+
+        # 7. MoE (Mixture of Experts)
+        moe_kws = ["moe", "mixture-of-experts", "expert"]
+        if any(kw in model_id_lower for kw in moe_kws) or "moe" in tags_lower:
+            use_cases.append("MoE")
+
+        # 8. General (Chat / Instruct)
+        chat_kws = ["chat", "instruct", "conversational", "it"]
+        if (

+
+    def _calculate_radar_score(self, item, prev_model):

+
+        # 1. Popularity Score (35%)
+        # 繝繧ｦ繝ｳ繝ｭ繝ｼ繝画焚縺ｨLike謨ｰ繧貞ｯｾ謨ｰ繧ｹ繧ｱ繝ｼ繝ｫ縺ｧ0-100縺ｫ豁｣隕丞喧
+        downloads = item["downloads"]
+        likes = item["likes"]
+
+        # 譛螟ｧ蛟､繧貞ｮ夂ｾｩ縺励※繧ｹ繧ｱ繝ｼ繝ｫ
+        max_dl_log = math.log10(1000000 + 1)  # 1M downloads
+        max_likes_log = math.log10(10000 + 1)  # 10K likes
+
+        dl_score = min(100.0, (math.log10(downloads + 1) / max_dl_log) * 100.0)
+        likes_score = min(100.0, (math.log10(likes + 1) / max_likes_log) * 100.0)
+
+        popularity_score = dl_score * 0.7 + likes_score * 0.3
+
+        # 2. Growth Score (25%)
+        # item 蜀・↓譬ｼ邏肴ｸ医∩縺ｮ growth_rate 繧貞茜逕ｨ
+        growth_rate = item.get("growth_rate", 0.0)
+        growth_score = min(100.0, growth_rate * 333.3)
body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Clamp negative growth scores to zero**

When both downloads and likes are lower than the previous snapshot, `growth_rate` becomes negative and this line turns the 25% growth component into a penalty rather than a zero contribution. That can happen when counters move down between daily collections, causing Radar Score to fall outside the intended 0窶・00 component model and mis-rank otherwise comparable models; clamp the computed growth score with a lower bound of `0.0`.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter non-LLM Hugging Face results before emitting**

At this append point every model returned by the broad `gguf` and search queries is included, regardless of pipeline or task. In the generated `models.json` I checked, this admits non-LLM entries such as `jonatasgrosman/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-japanese` and `cybersectony/phishing-email-detection-distilbert_v2.4.1`, so ASR/classifier models can appear in Explorer and inflate dashboard totals for a local LLM catalog; skip unsupported pipeline tags/tasks before adding the result.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

+                            continue
+                        # 驥崎､・・蠕檎ｶ壹〒繝槭・繧ｸ縺輔ｌ縲√ム繧ｦ繝ｳ繝ｭ繝ｼ繝画焚縺悟､壹＞繧ｯ繧ｨ繝ｪ縺ｮ邨先棡縺悟━蜈医＆繧後ｋ繧医≧縺ｫ荳頑嶌縺阪☆繧九°縲∽ｿ晄戟縺吶ｋ
+                        # downloads 繧・likes 縺悟､壹＞譁ｹ繧貞━蜈医＠縺ｦ菫晄戟
+                        existing = models_map.get(model_id)
+                        if not existing or model.get("downloads", 0) > existing.get(
+                            "downloads", 0
+                        ):
+                            models_map[model_id] = model
+                else:
+                    print(
+                        f"HF API returned status {response.status_code} for query {q}"
+                    )
+            except Exception as e:
+                print(f"Error collecting Hugging Face models for query {q}: {e}")
body.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Abort collection when a Hugging Face query fails**

If one Hugging Face query times out, this handler only logs it and returns whatever the other queries collected; because `collect_all.py` only aborts below 20 HF rows, a partial result set can still overwrite `data/latest` and drop whole families from the public dashboard. For scheduled collection, propagate per-query failures or compare against the previous count before writing so transient API errors do not corrupt the dataset.

Useful? React with 総ﾂ/ 綜.

**<sub><sub>![P3 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P3-lightgrey?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid deploying twice after each collection**

The deploy workflow already runs on pushes to `main`, and the collect workflow commits and pushes updated data, so this `workflow_run` trigger starts a second Pages build for the same collection; it also fires for completed failed collections unless gated on `conclusion == 'success'`. Remove this trigger or add a success condition to avoid duplicate/stale Pages deployments.
